accurate.why: All claims match the bill text (CRA disapproval, EPA rule title, Fed. Reg. citation, dates) and the contextual claim about a methane charge on petroleum/natural gas systems is verifiably accurate.

no_hallucinations.why: The added context (Congressional Review Act name, methane charge, EPA administration) is accurate and verified, not fabricated.

states_purpose.why: Clearly states the bill's purpose: disapproving the EPA's Waste Emissions Charge rule under the CRA.

changes_to_existing_law.why: Correctly explains the resolution nullifies the EPA rule so it has no force or effect under the CRA.

exceptions_conditions.why: The bill is a simple disapproval resolution with no exceptions or conditions, which the summary correctly notes.

effective_dates.why: Correctly states the law was approved March 14, 2025, and takes effect upon approval, with accurate passage dates.

major_provisions_covered.why: Captures the bill's sole operative effect: disapproving the identified rule so it has no force or effect.

objective_tone.why: The summary is neutral and descriptive with no advocacy or editorializing.

coherent.why: The summary is well-organized and internally consistent.

concise.why: The summary is appropriately brief without padding or repetition.

correct_entities.why: Correctly identifies the EPA, the Congressional Review Act, and the rule title.

correct_figures.why: All figures (89 Fed. Reg. 91094, November 18, 2024, and the 2025 dates) match the bill text exactly.
